query:
You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime repository.
The codebase is at version 0.6 (commit: 670a9140).

## Issue to Resolve

A number of bugs and inconsistencies with Java
https://gist.github.com/ilma4/a5ad5b5c284969e24f4e2149a2f8b273 lists several bugs and inconsistencies.

- [ ] `kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate.parse(s) vs LocalDate.Formats.ISO.parse(s)` (and the same for `LocalDateTime`): Java's facilities don't support having too many leading zeroes, but our parsing mechanism does, and delegating to Java prevents us from reading strings like `+00000000000002022-07-16`.
- [ ] `DatePeriod.parse and DateTimePeriod.parse parsing "P"`: it is inconsistent with ISO 8601 to parse just `P` without any components, so we should probably fix that.
- [x] `kotlinx.datetime.DatePeriod is always normalized unlike `java.time.Duration`: this is intentional.
- [x] `kotlinx.datetime.Instant.parse`: intentional.
- [x] `Different periods between two dates in Java and Kotlin`: looks like Java's bug.
- [x] `kotlin.datetime.Duration max value is different from Java's`: intentional.
- [ ] `kotlin.time.Duration.parseIsoString with too many digits`: it is a bug that we think `PT0000000000000000000000000M` is an infinite duration. (Not this library's scope, though)
- [ ] `kotlin.time.Duration.parse("PT+-2H") successfully parses -2 hours`: not valid ISO 8601, we can fix that. (Not this library's scope, though)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/pull/5370 is the `kotlin.time.Duration` portion of the fixes.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `core/common/src/DateTimePeriod.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/util.kt`
- `core/commonJs/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/commonJs/src/LocalDateTime.kt`
- `core/jvm/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/jvm/src/LocalDateTime.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2020 JetBrains s.r.o.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→@file:JvmName("LocalDateJvmKt")
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeAdd
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 13→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 14→import java.time.DateTimeException
 15→import java.time.format.DateTimeParseException
 16→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
 17→import java.time.LocalDate as jtLocalDate
 18→
 19→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 20→public actual class LocalDate internal constructor(internal val value: jtLocalDate) : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 21→    public actual companion object {
 22→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): LocalDate =
 23→            if (format === Formats.ISO) {
 24→                try {
 25→                    jtLocalDate.parse(input).let(::LocalDate)
 26→                } catch (e: DateTimeParseException) {
 27→                    throw DateTimeFormatException(e)
 28→                }
 29→            } else {
 30→                format.parse(input)
 31→            }
 32→
 33→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 34→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalDate = parse(input = isoString)
 35→
 36→        internal actual val MIN: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MIN)
 37→        internal actual val MAX: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MAX)
 38→
 39→        public actual fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate =
 40→            LocalDate(jtLocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDays.toLong()))
 41→
 42→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 43→        public actual fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> =
 44→            LocalDateFormat.build(block)
 45→    }
 46→
 47→    public actual object Formats {
 48→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> get() = ISO_DATE
 49→
 50→        public actual val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = ISO_DATE_BASIC
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    public actual constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) :
 54→            this(try {
 55→                jtLocalDate.of(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth)
 56→            } catch (e: DateTimeException) {
 57→                throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
 58→            })
 59→
 60→    public actual constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int) : this(year, month.number, dayOfMonth)
 61→
 62→    public actual val year: Int get() = value.year
 63→    public actual val monthNumber: Int get() = value.monthValue
 64→    public actual val month: Month get() = value.month
 65→    public actual val dayOfMonth: Int get() = value.dayOfMonth
 66→    public actual val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek get() = value.dayOfWeek
 67→    public actual val dayOfYear: Int get() = value.dayOfYear
 68→
 69→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 70→            (this === other) || (other is LocalDate && this.value == other.value)
 71→
 72→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
 73→
 74→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
 75→
 76→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
 77→
 78→    public actual fun toEpochDays(): Int = value.toEpochDay().clampToInt()
 79→}
 80→
 81→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
 82→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 83→        plus(1L, unit)
 84→
 85→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 86→        plus(value.toLong(), unit)
 87→
 88→public actual fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 89→        plus(-value.toLong(), unit)
 90→
 91→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 92→        try {
 93→            when (unit) {
 94→                is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> {
 95→                    val addDays: Long = safeMultiply(value, unit.days.toLong())
 96→                    ofEpochDayChecked(safeAdd(this.value.toEpochDay(), addDays))
 97→                }
 98→                is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased ->
 99→                    this.value.plusMonths(safeMultiply(value, unit.months.toLong()))
100→            }.let(::LocalDate)
101→        } catch (e: Exception) {
102→            if (e !is DateTimeException && e !is ArithmeticException) throw e
103→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value of $unit to $this is out of LocalDate range.", e)
104→        }
105→
106→private val minEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MIN.toEpochDay()
107→private val maxEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MAX.toEpochDay()
108→private fun ofEpochDayChecked(epochDay: Long): java.time.LocalDate {
109→    // LocalDate.ofEpochDay doesn't actually check that the argument doesn't overflow year calculation
110→    if (epochDay !in minEpochDay..maxEpochDay)
111→        throw DateTimeException("The resulting day $epochDay is out of supported LocalDate range.")
112→    return java.time.LocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDay)
113→}
114→
115→public actual operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate = try {
116→    with(period) {
117→        return@with value
118→                .run { if (totalMonths != 0) plusMonths(totalMonths.toLong()) else this }
119→                .run { if (days != 0) plusDays(days.toLong()) else this }
120→
121→    }.let(::LocalDate)
122→} catch (e: DateTimeException) {
123→    throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value to $this is out of LocalDate range.")
124→}
125→
126→
127→public actual fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod {
128→    var startD = this.value
129→    val endD = other.value
130→    val months = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.MONTHS); startD = startD.plusMonths(months)
131→    val days = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.DAYS)
132→
133→    if (months > Int.MAX_VALUE || months < Int.MIN_VALUE) {
134→        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The number of months between $this and $other does not fit in an Int")
135→    }
136→    return DatePeriod(totalMonths = months.toInt(), days.toInt())
137→}
138→
139→public actual fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int = when(unit) {
140→    is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS) / unit.months).clampToInt()
141→    is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS) / unit.days).clampToInt()
142→}
143→
144→public actual fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
145→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS).clampToInt()
146→
147→public actual fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
148→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS).clampToInt()
149→
150→public actual fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
151→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.YEARS).clampToInt()
152→

 15→/**

 21→ * You can also create custom instances using the constructor.
 22→ *
 23→ * An [IllegalArgumentException] will be thrown if some month name is empty or there are duplicate names.
 24→ *
 25→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.MonthNamesSamples.usage
 26→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.MonthNamesSamples.constructionFromList
 27→ */
 28→public class MonthNames(
 29→    /**
 30→     * A list of month names in order from January to December.
 31→     *
 32→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.MonthNamesSamples.names
 33→     */
 34→    public val names: List<String>
 35→) {
 36→    init {
 37→        require(names.size == 12) { "Month names must contain exactly 12 elements" }
 38→        names.indices.forEach { ix ->
 39→            require(names[ix].isNotEmpty()) { "A month name can not be empty" }
 40→            for (ix2 in 0 until ix) {
 41→                require(names[ix] != names[ix2]) {
 42→                    "Month names must be unique, but '${names[ix]}' was repeated"
 43→                }
 44→            }
 45→        }
 46→    }
 47→
 48→    /**

 64→         */
 65→        public val ENGLISH_FULL: MonthNames = MonthNames(

192→internal fun <T> requireParsedField(field: T?, name: String): T {
193→    if (field == null) {
194→        throw DateTimeFormatException("Can not create a $name from the given input: the field $name is missing")
195→    }
196→    return field
197→}

199→internal interface DateFieldContainer {
200→    var year: Int?
201→    var monthNumber: Int?
202→    var dayOfMonth: Int?
203→    var isoDayOfWeek: Int?
204→}
205→
206→private object DateFields {
207→    val year = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::year))
208→    val month = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::monthNumber), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
209→    val dayOfMonth = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::dayOfMonth), minValue = 1, maxValue = 31)
210→    val isoDayOfWeek = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::isoDayOfWeek), minValue = 1, maxValue = 7)
211→}
212→
213→/**
214→ * A [kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate], but potentially incomplete and inconsistent.
215→ */
216→internal class IncompleteLocalDate(
217→    override var year: Int? = null,
218→    override var monthNumber: Int? = null,
219→    override var dayOfMonth: Int? = null,
220→    override var isoDayOfWeek: Int? = null
221→) : DateFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalDate> {
222→    fun toLocalDate(): LocalDate {
223→        val date = LocalDate(
224→            requireParsedField(year, "year"),
225→            requireParsedField(monthNumber, "monthNumber"),
226→            requireParsedField(dayOfMonth, "dayOfMonth")
227→        )
228→        isoDayOfWeek?.let {
229→            if (it != date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber) {
230→                throw DateTimeFormatException(
231→                    "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
232→                        "the day of week is ${DayOfWeek(it)} but the date is $date, which is a ${date.dayOfWeek}"
233→                )
234→            }
235→        }
236→        return date
237→    }
238→
239→    fun populateFrom(date: LocalDate) {
240→        year = date.year
241→        monthNumber = date.monthNumber
242→        dayOfMonth = date.dayOfMonth
243→        isoDayOfWeek = date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber
244→    }
245→
246→    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, isoDayOfWeek)
247→
248→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
249→        other is IncompleteLocalDate && year == other.year && monthNumber == other.monthNumber &&
250→            dayOfMonth == other.dayOfMonth && isoDayOfWeek == other.isoDayOfWeek
251→
252→    override fun hashCode(): Int =
253→        year.hashCode() * 31 + monthNumber.hashCode() * 31 + dayOfMonth.hashCode() * 31 + isoDayOfWeek.hashCode() * 31
254→
255→    override fun toString(): String =
256→        "${year ?: "??"}-${monthNumber ?: "??"}-${dayOfMonth ?: "??"} (day of week is ${isoDayOfWeek ?: "??"})"
257→}
258→
259→private class YearDirective(private val padding: Padding, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
260→    SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
261→        DateFields.year,
262→        minDigits = padding.minDigits(4),
263→        maxDigits = null,
264→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(4),
265→        outputPlusOnExceededWidth = 4,
266→    ) {
267→    override val builderRepresentation: String
268→        get() = when (padding) {
269→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}()"
270→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
271→        }.let {
272→            if (isYearOfEra) {
273→                it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
274→            } else it
275→        }
276→
277→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
278→        other is YearDirective && padding == other.padding && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
279→
280→    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
281→}
282→
283→private class ReducedYearDirective(val base: Int, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
284→    ReducedIntFieldDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
285→        DateFields.year,
286→        digits = 2,
287→        base = base,
288→    ) {
289→    override val builderRepresentation: String
290→        get() =
291→            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::yearTwoDigits.name}($base)".let {
292→                if (isYearOfEra) {
293→                    it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
294→                } else it
295→            }
296→
297→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
298→        other is ReducedYearDirective && base == other.base && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
299→
300→    override fun hashCode(): Int = base.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
301→}
302→
303→private const val YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT =
304→    " /** TODO: the original format had an `y` directive, so the behavior is different on years earlier than 1 AD. See the [kotlinx.datetime.format.byUnicodePattern] documentation for details. */"
305→
306→/**
307→ * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
308→ * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
309→ * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
310→ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
311→ */
312→internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEra(padding: Padding) {
313→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
314→    when (this) {
315→        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
316→            BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding, isYearOfEra = true))
317→        )
318→    }
319→}
320→
321→/**
322→ * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
323→ * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
324→ * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
325→ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
326→ */
327→internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEraTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) {
328→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
329→    when (this) {
330→        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
331→            BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear, isYearOfEra = true))
332→        )
333→    }
334→}
335→
336→private class MonthDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
337→    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
338→        DateFields.month,
339→        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
340→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2),
341→    ) {
342→    override val builderRepresentation: String
343→        get() = when (padding) {
344→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthNumber.name}()"
345→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthNumber.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
346→        }
347→
348→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MonthDirective && padding == other.padding
349→    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
350→}
351→
352→private class MonthNameDirective(private val names: MonthNames) :
353→    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.month, names.names, "monthName") {
354→    override val builderRepresentation: String
355→        get() =
356→            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthName.name}(${names.toKotlinCode()})"
357→
358→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MonthNameDirective && names.names == other.names.names
359→    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()
360→}
361→
362→private class DayDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
363→    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
364→        DateFields.dayOfMonth,
365→        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
366→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2),
367→    ) {
368→    override val builderRepresentation: String
369→        get() = when (padding) {
370→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfMonth.name}()"
371→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfMonth.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
372→        }
373→
374→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayDirective && padding == other.padding
375→    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
376→}
377→
378→private class DayOfWeekDirective(private val names: DayOfWeekNames) :
379→    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.isoDayOfWeek, names.names, "dayOfWeekName") {
380→
381→    override val builderRepresentation: String
382→        get() =
383→            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfWeek.name}(${names.toKotlinCode()})"
384→
385→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayOfWeekDirective && names.names == other.names.names
386→    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()
387→}
388→
389→internal class LocalDateFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
390→    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalDate, IncompleteLocalDate>() {
391→    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalDate): IncompleteLocalDate =
392→        IncompleteLocalDate().apply { populateFrom(value) }
393→
394→    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalDate): LocalDate = intermediate.toLocalDate()
395→
396→    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyIncompleteLocalDate
397→
398→    companion object {
399→        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> {
400→            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
401→            builder.block()
402→            return LocalDateFormat(builder.build())
403→        }
404→    }
405→
406→    internal class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
407→        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithDateBuilder {
408→
409→        override fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) =
410→            actualBuilder.add(structure)
411→
412→        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
413→    }
414→}
415→
416→internal interface AbstractWithDateBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate {
417→    fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>)
418→
419→    override fun year(padding: Padding) =
420→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding)))
421→
422→    override fun yearTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) =
423→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear)))
424→
425→    override fun monthNumber(padding: Padding) =
426→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthDirective(padding)))
427→
428→    override fun monthName(names: MonthNames) =
429→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthNameDirective(names)))
430→
431→    override fun dayOfMonth(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayDirective(padding)))
432→    override fun dayOfWeek(names: DayOfWeekNames) =
433→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayOfWeekDirective(names)))
434→
435→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
436→    override fun date(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>) = when (format) {
437→        is LocalDateFormat -> addFormatStructureForDate(format.actualFormat)
438→    }
439→}
440→
441→// these are constants so that the formats are not recreated every time they are used
442→internal val ISO_DATE by lazy {
443→    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); char('-'); monthNumber(); char('-'); dayOfMonth() }
444→}
445→internal val ISO_DATE_BASIC by lazy {
446→    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); monthNumber(); dayOfMonth() }
447→}
448→
449→private val emptyIncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate()
450→

 5→@file:JvmName("LocalDateTimeJvmKt")
 6→package kotlinx.datetime
 7→
 8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer
10→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
11→import java.time.DateTimeException
12→import java.time.format.DateTimeParseException
13→import java.time.LocalDateTime as jtLocalDateTime
14→
15→public actual typealias Month = java.time.Month
16→public actual typealias DayOfWeek = java.time.DayOfWeek
17→
18→@Serializable(with = LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
19→public actual class LocalDateTime internal constructor(internal val value: jtLocalDateTime) : Comparable<LocalDateTime> {
20→
21→    public actual constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int, hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int, nanosecond: Int) :
22→            this(try {
23→                jtLocalDateTime.of(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
24→            } catch (e: DateTimeException) {
25→                throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
26→            })
27→
28→    public actual constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int, hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int, nanosecond: Int) :
29→            this(year, month.number, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
30→
31→    public actual constructor(date: LocalDate, time: LocalTime) :
32→            this(jtLocalDateTime.of(date.value, time.value))
33→
34→    public actual val year: Int get() = value.year
35→    public actual val monthNumber: Int get() = value.monthValue
36→    public actual val month: Month get() = value.month
37→    public actual val dayOfMonth: Int get() = value.dayOfMonth
38→    public actual val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek get() = value.dayOfWeek
39→    public actual val dayOfYear: Int get() = value.dayOfYear
40→
41→    public actual val hour: Int get() = value.hour
42→    public actual val minute: Int get() = value.minute
43→    public actual val second: Int get() = value.second
44→    public actual val nanosecond: Int get() = value.nano
45→
46→    public actual val date: LocalDate get() = LocalDate(value.toLocalDate()) // cache?
47→
48→    public actual val time: LocalTime get() = LocalTime(value.toLocalTime())
49→
50→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
51→            (this === other) || (other is LocalDateTime && this.value == other.value)
52→
53→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
54→
55→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
56→
57→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDateTime): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
58→
59→    public actual companion object {
60→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>): LocalDateTime =
61→            if (format === Formats.ISO) {
62→                try {
63→                    jtLocalDateTime.parse(input).let(::LocalDateTime)
64→                } catch (e: DateTimeParseException) {
65→                    throw DateTimeFormatException(e)
66→                }
67→            } else {
68→                format.parse(input)
69→            }
70→
71→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
72→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalDateTime = parse(input = isoString)
73→
74→        internal actual val MIN: LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(jtLocalDateTime.MIN)
75→        internal actual val MAX: LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(jtLocalDateTime.MAX)
76→
77→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
78→        public actual fun Format(builder: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime> =
79→            LocalDateTimeFormat.build(builder)
80→    }
81→
82→    public actual object Formats {
83→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime> = ISO_DATETIME
84→    }
85→
86→}
87→
88→

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2020 JetBrains s.r.o.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodComponentSerializer
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodComponentSerializer
 13→import kotlin.math.*
 14→import kotlin.time.Duration
 15→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 16→
 17→/**
 18→ * A difference between two [instants][Instant], decomposed into date and time components.
 19→ *
 20→ * The date components are: [years] ([DateTimeUnit.YEAR]), [months] ([DateTimeUnit.MONTH]), and [days] ([DateTimeUnit.DAY]).
 21→ *
 22→ * The time components are: [hours] ([DateTimeUnit.HOUR]), [minutes] ([DateTimeUnit.MINUTE]),
 23→ * [seconds] ([DateTimeUnit.SECOND]), and [nanoseconds] ([DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND]).
 24→ *
 25→ * The time components are not independent and are always normalized together.
 26→ * Likewise, months are normalized together with years.
 27→ * For example, there is no difference between `DateTimePeriod(months = 24, hours = 2, minutes = 63)` and
 28→ * `DateTimePeriod(years = 2, hours = 3, minutes = 3)`.
 29→ *
 30→ * All components can also be negative: for example, `DateTimePeriod(months = -5, days = 6, hours = -3)`.
 31→ * Whereas `months = 5` means "5 months after," `months = -5` means "5 months earlier."
 32→ *
 33→ * A constant time interval that consists of a single non-zero component (like "yearly" or "quarterly") should be
 34→ * represented by a [DateTimeUnit] directly instead of a [DateTimePeriod]:
 35→ * for example, instead of `DateTimePeriod(months = 6)`, one could use `DateTimeUnit.MONTH * 6`.
 36→ * This provides a wider variety of operations: for example, finding how many such intervals fit between two instants
 37→ * or dates or adding a multiple of such intervals at once.
 38→ *
 39→ * ### Interaction with other entities
 40→ *
 41→ * [DateTimePeriod] can be returned from [Instant.periodUntil], representing the difference between two instants.
 42→ * Conversely, there is an [Instant.plus] overload that accepts a [DateTimePeriod] and returns a new instant.
 43→ *
 44→ * [DatePeriod] is a subtype of [DateTimePeriod] that only stores the date components and has all time components equal
 45→ * to zero.
 46→ *
 47→ * [DateTimePeriod] can be thought of as a combination of a [Duration] and a [DatePeriod], as it contains both the
 48→ * time components of [Duration] and the date components of [DatePeriod].
 49→ * [Duration.toDateTimePeriod] can be used to convert a [Duration] to the corresponding [DateTimePeriod].
 50→ *
 51→ * ### Construction, serialization, and deserialization
 52→ *
 53→ * When a [DateTimePeriod] is constructed in any way, a [DatePeriod] value, which is a subtype of [DateTimePeriod],
 54→ * will be returned if all time components happen to be zero.
 55→ *
 56→ * A `DateTimePeriod` can be constructed using the constructor function with the same name.
 57→ * See sample 1.
 58→ *
 59→ * [parse] and [toString] methods can be used to obtain a [DateTimePeriod] from and convert it to a string in the
 60→ * ISO 8601 extended format.
 61→ * See sample 2.
 62→ *
 63→ * `DateTimePeriod` can also be returned as the result of instant arithmetic operations (see [Instant.periodUntil]).
 64→ *
 65→ * Additionally, there are several `kotlinx-serialization` serializers for [DateTimePeriod]:
 66→ * - [DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer] for the ISO 8601 format;
 67→ * - [DateTimePeriodComponentSerializer]  for an object with components.
 68→ *
 69→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.construction
 70→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.simpleParsingAndFormatting
 71→ */
 72→@Serializable(with = DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
 73→// TODO: could be error-prone without explicitly named params
 74→public sealed class DateTimePeriod {
 75→    internal abstract val totalMonths: Int
 76→
 77→    /**
 78→     * The number of calendar days. Can be negative.
 79→     *
 80→     * Note that a calendar day is not identical to 24 hours, see [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
 81→     * Also, this field does not get normalized together with months, so values larger than 31 can be present.
 82→     *
 83→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
 84→     */
 85→    public abstract val days: Int
 86→    internal abstract val totalNanoseconds: Long
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * The number of whole years. Can be negative.
 90→     *
 91→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
 92→     */
 93→    public val years: Int get() = totalMonths / 12
 94→
 95→    /**
 96→     * The number of months in this period that don't form a whole year, so this value is always in `(-11..11)`.
 97→     *
 98→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
 99→     */
100→    public val months: Int get() = totalMonths % 12
101→
102→    /**
103→     * The number of whole hours in this period. Can be negative.
104→     *
105→     * This field does not get normalized together with days, so values larger than 23 or smaller than -23 can be present.
106→     *
107→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
108→     */
109→    public open val hours: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds / 3_600_000_000_000).toInt()
110→
111→    /**
112→     * The number of whole minutes in this period that don't form a whole hour, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
113→     *
114→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
115→     */
116→    public open val minutes: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 3_600_000_000_000) / 60_000_000_000).toInt()
117→
118→    /**
119→     * The number of whole seconds in this period that don't form a whole minute, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
120→     *
121→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
122→     */
123→    public open val seconds: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 60_000_000_000) / NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
124→
125→    /**
126→     * The number of whole nanoseconds in this period that don't form a whole second, so this value is always in
127→     * `(-999_999_999..999_999_999)`.
128→     *
129→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
130→     */
131→    public open val nanoseconds: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
132→
133→    private fun allNonpositive() =
134→        totalMonths <= 0 && days <= 0 && totalNanoseconds <= 0 && (totalMonths or days != 0 || totalNanoseconds != 0L)
135→
136→    /**
137→     * Converts this period to the ISO 8601 string representation for durations, for example, `P2M1DT3H`.
138→     *
139→     * Note that the ISO 8601 duration is not the same as [Duration],
140→     * but instead includes the date components, like [DateTimePeriod] does.
141→     *
142→     * Examples of the output:
143→     * - `P2Y4M-1D`: two years, four months, minus one day;
144→     * - `-P2Y4M1D`: minus two years, minus four months, minus one day;
145→     * - `P1DT3H2M4.123456789S`: one day, three hours, two minutes, four seconds, 123456789 nanoseconds;
146→     * - `P1DT-3H-2M-4.123456789S`: one day, minus three hours, minus two minutes,
147→     *   minus four seconds, minus 123456789 nanoseconds;
148→     *
149→     * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.5.2.2a)
150→     *
151→     * @see DateTimePeriod.parse for the detailed description of the format.
152→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.toStringSample
153→     */
154→    override fun toString(): String = buildString {
155→        val sign = if (allNonpositive()) { append('-'); -1 } else 1
156→        append('P')
157→        if (years != 0) append(years * sign).append('Y')
158→        if (months != 0) append(months * sign).append('M')
159→        if (days != 0) append(days * sign).append('D')
160→        var t = "T"
161→        if (hours != 0) append(t).append(hours * sign).append('H').also { t = "" }
162→        if (minutes != 0) append(t).append(minutes * sign).append('M').also { t = "" }
163→        if (seconds or nanoseconds != 0) {
164→            append(t)
165→            append(when {
166→                seconds != 0 -> seconds * sign
167→                nanoseconds * sign < 0 -> "-0"
168→                else -> "0"
169→            })
170→            if (nanoseconds != 0) append('.').append((nanoseconds.absoluteValue).toString().padStart(9, '0'))
171→            append('S')
172→        }
173→
174→        if (length == 1) append("0D")
175→    }
176→
177→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
178→        if (this === other) return true
179→        if (other !is DateTimePeriod) return false
180→
181→        if (totalMonths != other.totalMonths) return false
182→        if (days != other.days) return false
183→        if (totalNanoseconds != other.totalNanoseconds) return false
184→
185→        return true
186→    }
187→
188→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
189→        var result = totalMonths
190→        result = 31 * result + days
191→        result = 31 * result + totalNanoseconds.hashCode()
192→        return result
193→    }
194→
195→    public companion object {
196→        /**
197→         * Parses a ISO 8601 duration string as a [DateTimePeriod].
198→         *
199→         * If the time components are absent or equal to zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
200→         *
201→         * Note that the ISO 8601 duration is not the same as [Duration],
202→         * but instead includes the date components, like [DateTimePeriod] does.
203→         *
204→         * Examples of durations in the ISO 8601 format:
205→         * - `P1Y40D` is one year and 40 days
206→         * - `-P1DT1H` is minus (one day and one hour)
207→         * - `P1DT-1H` is one day minus one hour
208→         * - `-PT0.000000001S` is minus one nanosecond
209→         *
210→         * The format is defined as follows:
211→         * - First, optionally, a `-` or `+`.
212→         *   If `-` is present, the whole period after the `-` is negated: `-P-2M1D` is the same as `P2M-1D`.
213→         * - Then, the letter `P`.
214→         * - Optionally, the number of years, followed by `Y`.
215→         * - Optionally, the number of months, followed by `M`.
216→         * - Optionally, the number of weeks, followed by `W`.
217→         * - Optionally, the number of days, followed by `D`.
218→         * - The string can end here if there are no more time components.
219→         *   If there are time components, the letter `T` is required.
220→         * - Optionally, the number of hours, followed by `H`.
221→         * - Optionally, the number of minutes, followed by `M`.
222→         * - Optionally, the number of seconds, followed by `S`.
223→         *   Seconds can optionally have a fractional part with up to nine digits.
224→         *   The fractional part is separated with a `.`.
225→         *
226→         * An explicit `+` or `-` sign can be prepended to any number.
227→         * `-` means that the number is negative, and `+` has no effect.
228→         *
229→         * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.5.2.2a) and 5.5.2.2b).
230→         * We combine the two formats into one by allowing the number of weeks to go after the number of months
231→         * and before the number of days.
232→         *
233→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [DateTimePeriod] are
234→         * exceeded.
235→         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.parsing
236→         */
237→        public fun parse(text: String): DateTimePeriod {
238→            fun parseException(message: String, position: Int): Nothing =
239→                throw DateTimeFormatException("Parse error at char $position: $message")
240→            val START = 0
241→            val AFTER_P = 1
242→            val AFTER_YEAR = 2
243→            val AFTER_MONTH = 3
244→            val AFTER_WEEK = 4
245→            val AFTER_DAY = 5
246→            val AFTER_T = 6
247→            val AFTER_HOUR = 7
248→            val AFTER_MINUTE = 8
249→            val AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO = 9
250→
251→            var state = START
252→            // next unread character
253→            var i = 0
254→            var sign = 1
255→            var years = 0
256→            var months = 0
257→            var weeks = 0
258→            var days = 0
259→            var hours = 0
260→            var minutes = 0
261→            var seconds = 0
262→            var nanoseconds = 0
263→            while (true) {
264→                if (i >= text.length) {
265→                    if (state == START)
266→                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; 'P' designator is required", i)
267→                    if (state == AFTER_T)
268→                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; at least one time component is required after 'T'", i)
269→                    val daysTotal = when (val n = days.toLong() + weeks * 7) {
270→                        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> n.toInt()
271→                        else -> parseException("The total number of days under 'D' and 'W' designators should fit into an Int", 0)
272→                    }
273→                    return DateTimePeriod(years, months, daysTotal, hours, minutes, seconds, nanoseconds.toLong())
274→                }
275→                if (state == START) {
276→                    if (i + 1 >= text.length && (text[i] == '+' || text[i] == '-'))
277→                        parseException("Unexpected end of string; 'P' designator is required", i)
278→                    when (text[i]) {
279→                        '+', '-' -> {
280→                            if (text[i] == '-')
281→                                sign = -1
282→                            if (text[i + 1] != 'P')
283→                                parseException("Expected 'P', got '${text[i + 1]}'", i + 1)
284→                            i += 2
285→                        }
286→                        'P' -> { i += 1 }
287→                        else -> parseException("Expected '+', '-', 'P', got '${text[i]}'", i)
288→                    }
289→                    state = AFTER_P
290→                    continue
291→                }
292→                var localSign = sign
293→                val iStart = i
294→                when (text[i]) {
295→                    '+', '-' -> {
296→                        if (text[i] == '-') localSign *= -1
297→                        i += 1
298→                        if (i >= text.length || text[i] !in '0'..'9')
299→                            parseException("A number expected after '${text[i]}'", i)
300→                    }
301→                    in '0'..'9' -> { }
302→                    'T' -> {
303→                        if (state >= AFTER_T)
304→                            parseException("Only one 'T' designator is allowed", i)
305→                        state = AFTER_T
306→                        i += 1
307→                        continue
308→                    }
309→                }
310→                var number = 0L
311→                while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9') {
312→                    try {
313→                        number = safeAdd(safeMultiply(number, 10), (text[i] - '0').toLong())
314→                    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
315→                        parseException("The number is too large", iStart)
316→                    }
317→                    i += 1
318→                }
319→                number *= localSign
320→                if (i == text.length)
321→                    parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
322→                val wrongOrder = "Wrong component order: should be 'Y', 'M', 'W', 'D', then designator 'T', then 'H', 'M', 'S'"
323→                fun Long.toIntThrowing(component: Char): Int {
324→                    if (this < Int.MIN_VALUE || this > Int.MAX_VALUE)
325→                        parseException("Value $this does not fit into an Int, which is required for component '$component'", iStart)
326→                    return toInt()
327→                }
328→                when (text[i].uppercaseChar()) {
329→                    'Y' -> {
330→                        if (state >= AFTER_YEAR)
331→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
332→                        state = AFTER_YEAR
333→                        years = number.toIntThrowing('Y')
334→                    }
335→                    'M' -> {
336→                        if (state >= AFTER_T) {
337→                            // Minutes
338→                            if (state >= AFTER_MINUTE)
339→                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
340→                            state = AFTER_MINUTE
341→                            minutes = number.toIntThrowing('M')
342→                        } else {
343→                            // Months
344→                            if (state >= AFTER_MONTH)
345→                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
346→                            state = AFTER_MONTH
347→                            months = number.toIntThrowing('M')
348→                        }
349→                    }
350→                    'W' -> {
351→                        if (state >= AFTER_WEEK)
352→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
353→                        state = AFTER_WEEK
354→                        weeks = number.toIntThrowing('W')
355→                    }
356→                    'D' -> {
357→                        if (state >= AFTER_DAY)
358→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
359→                        state = AFTER_DAY
360→                        days = number.toIntThrowing('D')
361→                    }
362→                    'H' -> {
363→                        if (state >= AFTER_HOUR || state < AFTER_T)
364→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
365→                        state = AFTER_HOUR
366→                        hours = number.toIntThrowing('H')
367→                    }
368→                    'S' -> {
369→                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
370→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
371→                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
372→                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
373→                    }
374→                    '.', ',' -> {
375→                        i += 1
376→                        if (i >= text.length)
377→                            parseException("Expected designator 'S' after ${text[i - 1]}", i)
378→                        val iStartFraction = i
379→                        while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9')
380→                            i += 1
381→                        val fractionLength = i - iStartFraction
382→                        if (fractionLength > 9)
383→                            parseException("Only the nanosecond fractions of a second are supported", iStartFraction)
384→                        val fractionalPart = text.substring(iStartFraction, i) + "0".repeat(9 - fractionLength)
385→                        nanoseconds = fractionalPart.toInt(10) * localSign
386→                        if (text[i] != 'S')
387→                            parseException("Expected the 'S' designator after a fraction", i)
388→                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
389→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
390→                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
391→                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
392→                    }
393→                    else -> parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
394→                }
395→                i += 1
396→           }
397→        }
398→    }
399→}
400→
401→/**
402→ * @suppress
403→ */
404→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("DateTimePeriod.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
405→public fun String.toDateTimePeriod(): DateTimePeriod = DateTimePeriod.parse(this)
406→
407→/**
408→ * A special case of [DateTimePeriod] that only stores the date components and has all time components equal to zero.
409→ *
410→ * A `DatePeriod` is automatically returned from all constructor functions for [DateTimePeriod] if it turns out that
411→ * the time components are zero.
412→ *
413→ * ```
414→ * DateTimePeriod.parse("P1Y3D") as DatePeriod // 1 year and 3 days
415→ * ```
416→ *
417→ * Additionally, [DatePeriod] has its own constructor, the [parse] function that will fail if any of the time components
418→ * are not zero, and [DatePeriodIso8601Serializer] and [DatePeriodComponentSerializer], mirroring those of
419→ * [DateTimePeriod].
420→ *
421→ * `DatePeriod` values are used in operations on [LocalDates][LocalDate] and are returned from operations
422→ * on [LocalDates][LocalDate], but they also can be passed anywhere a [DateTimePeriod] is expected.
423→ *
424→ * On the JVM, there are `DatePeriod.toJavaPeriod()` and `java.time.Period.toKotlinDatePeriod()`
425→ * extension functions to convert between `kotlinx.datetime` and `java.time` objects used for the same purpose.
426→ *
427→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DatePeriodSamples.simpleParsingAndFormatting
428→ */
429→@Serializable(with = DatePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
430→public class DatePeriod internal constructor(
431→    internal override val totalMonths: Int,
432→    override val days: Int,
433→) : DateTimePeriod() {
434→    /**
435→     * Constructs a new [DatePeriod].
436→     *
437→     * It is recommended to always name the arguments explicitly when constructing this manually,
438→     * like `DatePeriod(years = 1, months = 12, days = 16)`.
439→     *
440→     * The passed numbers are not stored as is but are normalized instead for human readability, so, for example,
441→     * `DateTimePeriod(months = 24, days = 41)` becomes `DateTimePeriod(years = 2, days = 41)`.
442→     *
443→     * If only a single component is set and is always non-zero and is semantically a fixed time interval
444→     * (like "yearly" or "quarterly"), please consider using a multiple of [DateTimeUnit.DateBased] instead.
445→     * For example, instead of `DatePeriod(months = 6)`, one can use `DateTimeUnit.MONTH * 6`.
446→     *
447→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the total number of months in [years] and [months] overflows an [Int].
448→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DatePeriodSamples.construction
449→     */
450→    public constructor(years: Int = 0, months: Int = 0, days: Int = 0): this(totalMonths(years, months), days)
451→    // avoiding excessive computations
452→    /** The number of whole hours in this period. Always equal to zero. */
453→    override val hours: Int get() = 0
454→
455→    /** The number of whole minutes in this period. Always equal to zero. */
456→    override val minutes: Int get() = 0
457→
458→    /** The number of whole seconds in this period. Always equal to zero. */
459→    override val seconds: Int get() = 0
460→
461→    /** The number of nanoseconds in this period. Always equal to zero. */
462→    override val nanoseconds: Int get() = 0
463→    internal override val totalNanoseconds: Long get() = 0
464→
465→    public companion object {
466→        /**
467→         * Parses the ISO 8601 duration representation as a [DatePeriod], for example, `P1Y2M30D`.
468→         *
469→         * This function is equivalent to [DateTimePeriod.parse], but will fail if any of the time components are not
470→         * zero.
471→         *
472→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed, the boundaries of [DatePeriod] are exceeded,
473→         * or any time components are not zero.
474→         *
475→         * @see DateTimePeriod.parse
476→         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DatePeriodSamples.parsing
477→         */
478→        public fun parse(text: String): DatePeriod =
479→            when (val period = DateTimePeriod.parse(text)) {
480→                is DatePeriod -> period
481→                else -> throw DateTimeFormatException("Period $period (parsed from string $text) is not date-based")
482→            }
483→    }
484→}
485→
486→/**
487→ * @suppress
488→ */
489→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("DatePeriod.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
490→public fun String.toDatePeriod(): DatePeriod = DatePeriod.parse(this)
491→
492→private class DateTimePeriodImpl(
493→    internal override val totalMonths: Int,
494→    override val days: Int,
495→    internal override val totalNanoseconds: Long,
496→) : DateTimePeriod()
497→
498→// TODO: these calculations fit in a JS Number. Possible to do an expect/actual here.
499→private fun totalMonths(years: Int, months: Int): Int =
500→    when (val totalMonths = years.toLong() * 12 + months.toLong()) {
501→        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> totalMonths.toInt()
502→        else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of months in $years years and $months months overflows an Int")
503→    }
504→
505→private fun totalNanoseconds(hours: Int, minutes: Int, seconds: Int, nanoseconds: Long): Long {
506→    val totalMinutes: Long = hours.toLong() * 60 + minutes
507→    // absolute value at most 61 * Int.MAX_VALUE
508→    val totalMinutesAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutes * 60
509→    // absolute value at most 61 * 60 * Int.MAX_VALUE < 64 * 64 * 2^31 = 2^43
510→    val minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutesAsSeconds + nanoseconds / NANOS_PER_ONE
511→    // absolute value at most 2^43 + 2^63 / 10^9 < 2^43 + 2^34 < 2^44
512→    val totalSeconds = minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds + seconds
513→    // absolute value at most 2^44 + 2^31 < 2^45
514→    return try {
515→        multiplyAndAdd(totalSeconds, 1_000_000_000, nanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE)
516→    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
517→        throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of nanoseconds in $hours hours, $minutes minutes, $seconds seconds, and $nanoseconds nanoseconds overflows a Long")
518→    }
519→}
520→
521→internal fun buildDateTimePeriod(totalMonths: Int = 0, days: Int = 0, totalNanoseconds: Long): DateTimePeriod =
522→    if (totalNanoseconds != 0L)
523→        DateTimePeriodImpl(totalMonths, days, totalNanoseconds)
524→    else
525→        DatePeriod(totalMonths, days)
526→
527→/**
528→ * Constructs a new [DateTimePeriod]. If all the time components are zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
529→ *
530→ * It is recommended to always name the arguments explicitly when constructing this manually,
531→ * like `DateTimePeriod(years = 1, months = 12, days = 16)`.
532→ *
533→ * The passed numbers are not stored as is but are normalized instead for human readability, so, for example,
534→ * `DateTimePeriod(months = 24, days = 41)` becomes `DateTimePeriod(years = 2, days = 41)`.
535→ *
536→ * If only a single component is set and is always non-zero and is semantically a fixed time interval
537→ * (like "yearly" or "quarterly"), please consider using a multiple of [DateTimeUnit] instead.
538→ * For example, instead of `DateTimePeriod(months = 6)`, one can use `DateTimeUnit.MONTH * 6`.
539→ *
540→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the total number of months in [years] and [months] overflows an [Int].
541→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the total number of months in [hours], [minutes], [seconds] and [nanoseconds]
542→ * overflows a [Long].
543→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.constructorFunction
544→ */
545→public fun DateTimePeriod(
546→    years: Int = 0,
547→    months: Int = 0,
548→    days: Int = 0,
549→    hours: Int = 0,
550→    minutes: Int = 0,
551→    seconds: Int = 0,
552→    nanoseconds: Long = 0
553→): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(totalMonths(years, months), days,
554→    totalNanoseconds(hours, minutes, seconds, nanoseconds))
555→
556→/**
557→ * Constructs a [DateTimePeriod] from a [Duration].
558→ *
559→ * If the duration value is too big to be represented as a [Long] number of nanoseconds,
560→ * the result will be [Long.MAX_VALUE] nanoseconds.
561→ *
562→ * **Pitfall**: a [DateTimePeriod] obtained this way will always have zero date components.
563→ * The reason is that even a [Duration] obtained via [Duration.Companion.days] just means a multiple of 24 hours,
564→ * whereas in `kotlinx-datetime`, a day is a calendar day, which can be different from 24 hours.
565→ * See [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
566→ *
567→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.durationToDateTimePeriod
568→ */
569→// TODO: maybe it's more consistent to throw here on overflow?
570→public fun Duration.toDateTimePeriod(): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(totalNanoseconds = inWholeNanoseconds)
571→
572→/**
573→ * Adds two [DateTimePeriod] instances.
574→ *
575→ * **Pitfall**: given three instants, adding together the periods between the first and the second and between the
576→ * second and the third *does not* necessarily equal the period between the first and the third.
577→ *
578→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow happens.
579→ */
580→public operator fun DateTimePeriod.plus(other: DateTimePeriod): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(
581→    safeAdd(totalMonths, other.totalMonths),
582→    safeAdd(days, other.days),
583→    safeAdd(totalNanoseconds, other.totalNanoseconds),
584→)
585→
586→/**
587→ * Adds two [DatePeriod] instances.
588→ *
589→ * **Pitfall**: given three dates, adding together the periods between the first and the second and between the
590→ * second and the third *does not* necessarily equal the period between the first and the third.
591→ *
592→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow happens.
593→ */
594→public operator fun DatePeriod.plus(other: DatePeriod): DatePeriod = DatePeriod(
595→    safeAdd(totalMonths, other.totalMonths),
596→    safeAdd(days, other.days),
597→)
598→

 7→package kotlinx.datetime
 8→
 9→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
11→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalTimeIso8601Serializer
12→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
13→import java.time.DateTimeException
14→import java.time.format.DateTimeParseException
15→import java.time.LocalTime as jtLocalTime
16→
17→@Serializable(with = LocalTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
18→public actual class LocalTime internal constructor(internal val value: jtLocalTime) :

 14→/**
 15→ * The AM/PM marker that indicates whether the hour in range `1..12` is before or after noon.
 16→ */
 17→public enum class AmPmMarker {
 18→    /** The time is before noon. */
 19→    AM,
 20→
 21→    /** The time is after noon. */
 22→    PM,
 23→}
 24→
 25→internal interface TimeFieldContainer {
 26→    var minute: Int?
 27→    var second: Int?
 28→    var nanosecond: Int?
 29→    var hour: Int?
 30→    var hourOfAmPm: Int?
 31→    var amPm: AmPmMarker?
 32→
 33→    var fractionOfSecond: DecimalFraction?
 34→        get() = nanosecond?.let { DecimalFraction(it, 9) }
 35→        set(value) {
 36→            nanosecond = value?.fractionalPartWithNDigits(9)
 37→        }
 38→}

 40→private object TimeFields {
 41→    val hour = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hour), minValue = 0, maxValue = 23)
 42→    val minute = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::minute), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59)
 43→    val second =
 44→        UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::second), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59, defaultValue = 0)
 45→    val fractionOfSecond =
 46→        GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::fractionOfSecond), defaultValue = DecimalFraction(0, 9))
 47→    val amPm = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::amPm))
 48→    val hourOfAmPm = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hourOfAmPm), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
 49→}

 51→internal class IncompleteLocalTime(
 52→    override var hour: Int? = null,
 53→    override var hourOfAmPm: Int? = null,
 54→    override var amPm: AmPmMarker? = null,
 55→    override var minute: Int? = null,
 56→    override var second: Int? = null,
 57→    override var nanosecond: Int? = null
 58→) : TimeFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalTime> {
 59→    fun toLocalTime(): LocalTime {
 60→        val hour: Int = hour?.let { hour ->
 61→            hourOfAmPm?.let {
 62→                require((hour + 11) % 12 + 1 == it) { "Inconsistent hour and hour-of-am-pm: hour is $hour, but hour-of-am-pm is $it" }
 63→            }
 64→            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 65→                require((amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) == (hour >= 12)) {
 66→                    "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is $hour, but the AM/PM marker is $amPm"
 67→                }
 68→            }
 69→            hour
 70→        } ?: hourOfAmPm?.let { hourOfAmPm ->
 71→            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 72→                hourOfAmPm.let { if (it == 12) 0 else it } + if (amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) 12 else 0
 73→            }
 74→        } ?: throw DateTimeFormatException("Incomplete time: missing hour")
 75→        return LocalTime(
 76→            hour,
 77→            requireParsedField(minute, "minute"),
 78→            second ?: 0,
 79→            nanosecond ?: 0,
 80→        )
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    fun populateFrom(localTime: LocalTime) {
 84→        hour = localTime.hour
 85→        hourOfAmPm = (localTime.hour + 11) % 12 + 1
 86→        amPm = if (localTime.hour >= 12) AmPmMarker.PM else AmPmMarker.AM
 87→        minute = localTime.minute
 88→        second = localTime.second
 89→        nanosecond = localTime.nanosecond
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime(hour, hourOfAmPm, amPm, minute, second, nanosecond)
 93→
 94→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 95→        other is IncompleteLocalTime && hour == other.hour && hourOfAmPm == other.hourOfAmPm && amPm == other.amPm &&
 96→            minute == other.minute && second == other.second && nanosecond == other.nanosecond

100→            (second ?: 0) * 31 + (nanosecond ?: 0)
101→
102→    override fun toString(): String =
103→        "${hour ?: "??"}:${minute ?: "??"}:${second ?: "??"}.${
104→            nanosecond?.let { nanos ->
105→                nanos.toString().let { it.padStart(9 - it.length, '0') }
106→            } ?: "???"
107→        }"
108→}

215→internal class FractionalSecondDirective(
216→    private val minDigits: Int,
217→    private val maxDigits: Int,
218→    zerosToAdd: List<Int> = NO_EXTRA_ZEROS,
219→) :
220→    DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
221→        TimeFields.fractionOfSecond,
222→        minDigits,
223→        maxDigits,
224→        zerosToAdd
225→    ) {
226→
227→    override val builderRepresentation: String
228→        get() {
229→            val ref = "secondFraction" // can't directly reference `secondFraction` due to resolution ambiguity
230→            // we ignore `grouping`, as it's not representable in the end users' code
231→            return when {
232→                minDigits == 1 && maxDigits == 9 -> "$ref()"
233→                minDigits == 1 -> "$ref(maxLength = $maxDigits)"
234→                maxDigits == 1 -> "$ref(minLength = $minDigits)"
235→                maxDigits == minDigits -> "$ref($minDigits)"
236→                else -> "$ref($minDigits, $maxDigits)"

238→        }

240→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
241→        other is FractionalSecondDirective && minDigits == other.minDigits && maxDigits == other.maxDigits

245→    companion object {

247→        val GROUP_BY_THREE = listOf(2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0)

  6→package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format

  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.DecimalFraction
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.formatter.*
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * A directive that specifies a way to parse and format the [field].
 14→ */
 15→internal interface FieldFormatDirective<in Target> {
 16→    /**
 17→     * The field parsed and formatted by this directive.
 18→     */
 19→    val field: FieldSpec<Target, *>
 20→
 21→    /**
 22→     * The formatter operation that formats the field.
 23→     */
 24→    fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target>
 25→
 26→    /**
 27→     * The parser structure that parses the field.
 28→     */
 29→    fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target>
 30→
 31→    /**
 32→     * The string with the code that, when evaluated in the builder context, appends the directive.
 33→     */
 34→    val builderRepresentation: String
 35→}
 36→
 37→/**
 38→ * A directive for a decimal format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 39→ * The field is formatted with the field padded to [minDigits] with zeroes,
 40→ * and the parser expects the field to be at least [minDigits] digits long.
 41→ */
 42→internal abstract class UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 43→    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 44→    private val minDigits: Int,
 45→    private val spacePadding: Int?,
 46→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 47→
 48→    private val maxDigits: Int = field.maxDigits
 49→
 50→    init {
 51→        require(minDigits >= 0) {
 52→            "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative"
 53→        }
 54→        require(maxDigits >= minDigits) {
 55→            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 56→        }
 57→        if (spacePadding != null) {
 58→            require(spacePadding > minDigits) {
 59→                "The space padding ($spacePadding) should be more than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
 65→        val formatter = UnsignedIntFormatterStructure(
 66→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
 67→            zeroPadding = minDigits,
 68→        )
 69→        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
 73→        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, field.accessor, field.name)
 74→}
 75→
 76→/**
 77→ * A directive for a string-based format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 78→ */
 79→internal abstract class NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 80→    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 81→    private val values: List<String>,
 82→    private val name: String,
 83→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 84→
 85→    init {
 86→        require(values.size == field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1) {
 87→            "The number of values (${values.size}) in $values does not match the range of the field (${field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1})"
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
 92→        values.getOrNull(it-field.minValue)
 93→            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
 97→        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
 98→            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, values.indexOf(newValue) + field.minValue)?.let {
 99→                values[it - field.minValue]
100→            }
101→
102→        override val name: String get() = this@NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective.name
103→    }
104→
105→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
106→        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
107→
108→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
109→        ParserStructure(
110→            listOf(
111→                StringSetParserOperation(values, AssignableString(), "one of $values for $name")
112→            ), emptyList()
113→        )
114→}
115→
116→/**
117→ * A directive for a string-based format of an enum field.
118→ */
119→internal abstract class NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target, Type>(
120→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Type>,
121→    private val mapping: Map<Type, String>,
122→    private val name: String,
123→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
124→
125→    private val reverseMapping = mapping.entries.associate { it.value to it.key }
126→
127→    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
128→        mapping[field.accessor.getterNotNull(target)]
129→            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
130→    }
131→
132→    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
133→        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
134→            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, reverseMapping[newValue]!!)?.let { mapping[it] }
135→
136→        override val name: String get() = this@NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective.name
137→    }
138→
139→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
140→        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
141→
142→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
143→        ParserStructure(
144→            listOf(
145→                StringSetParserOperation(mapping.values, AssignableString(), "one of ${mapping.values} for $name")
146→            ), emptyList()
147→        )
148→}
149→
150→internal abstract class StringFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
151→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, String>,
152→    private val acceptedStrings: Set<String>,
153→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
154→
155→    init {
156→        require(acceptedStrings.isNotEmpty()) {
157→            "The set of accepted strings is empty"
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
162→        StringFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull)
163→
164→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
165→        ParserStructure(
166→            listOf(StringSetParserOperation(acceptedStrings, field.accessor, field.name)),
167→            emptyList()
168→        )
169→}
170→
171→internal abstract class SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
172→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
173→    private val minDigits: Int?,
174→    private val maxDigits: Int?,
175→    private val spacePadding: Int?,
176→    private val outputPlusOnExceededWidth: Int?,
177→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
178→
179→    init {
180→        require(minDigits == null || minDigits >= 0) { "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative" }
181→        require(maxDigits == null || minDigits == null || maxDigits >= minDigits) {
182→            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
183→        }
184→    }
185→
186→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
187→        val formatter = SignedIntFormatterStructure(
188→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
189→            zeroPadding = minDigits ?: 0,
190→            outputPlusOnExceededWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
191→        )
192→        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
193→    }
194→
195→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
196→        SignedIntParser(
197→            minDigits = minDigits,
198→            maxDigits = maxDigits,
199→            spacePadding = spacePadding,
200→            field.accessor,
201→            field.name,
202→            plusOnExceedsWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
203→        )
204→}
205→
206→internal abstract class DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
207→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, DecimalFraction>,
208→    private val minDigits: Int,
209→    private val maxDigits: Int,
210→    private val zerosToAdd: List<Int>,
211→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
212→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
213→        DecimalFractionFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull, minDigits, maxDigits, zerosToAdd)
214→
215→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> = ParserStructure(
216→        listOf(
217→            NumberSpanParserOperation(
218→                listOf(FractionPartConsumer(minDigits, maxDigits, field.accessor, field.name))
219→            )
220→        ),
221→        emptyList()
222→    )
223→}
224→
225→internal abstract class ReducedIntFieldDirective<in Target>(
226→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
227→    private val digits: Int,
228→    private val base: Int,
229→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
230→
231→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
232→        ReducedIntFormatterStructure(
233→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
234→            digits = digits,
235→            base = base,
236→        )
237→
238→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
239→        ReducedIntParser(digits = digits, base = base, field.accessor, field.name)
240→}

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.AssignableField
  9→import kotlin.reflect.KMutableProperty1
 10→
 11→/**
 12→ * A lens-like interface for accessing fields of objects.
 13→ */
 14→internal interface Accessor<in Object, Field>: AssignableField<Object, Field> {
 15→    /**
 16→     * The value of the field in the given object, or `null` if the field is not set.
 17→     */
 18→    fun getter(container: Object): Field?
 19→
 20→    /**
 21→     * Function that returns the value of the field in the given object,
 22→     * or throws [IllegalStateException] if the field is not set.
 23→     *
 24→     * This function is used to access fields during formatting.
 25→     */
 26→    fun getterNotNull(container: Object): Field =
 27→        getter(container) ?: throw IllegalStateException("Field $name is not set")
 28→}
 29→
 30→/**
 31→ * An implementation of [Accessor] for a mutable property of an object.
 32→ */
 33→internal class PropertyAccessor<Object, Field>(private val property: KMutableProperty1<Object, Field?>): Accessor<Object, Field> {
 34→    override val name: String get() = property.name
 35→
 36→    override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Object, newValue: Field): Field? {
 37→        val oldValue = property.get(container)
 38→        return when {
 39→            oldValue === null -> {
 40→                property.set(container, newValue)
 41→                null
 42→            }
 43→            oldValue == newValue -> null
 44→            else -> oldValue
 45→        }
 46→    }
 47→
 48→    override fun getter(container: Object): Field? = property.get(container)
 49→}
 50→
 51→/**
 52→ * A description of the field's numeric sign.
 53→ *
 54→ * Several fields can share the same sign. For example, the hour and the minute of the UTC offset have the same sign,
 55→ * and setting the sign of the hour also sets the sign of the minute.
 56→ *
 57→ * Implementations of this interface are *not* required to redefine [equals] and [hashCode].
 58→ * Instead, signs should be defined as singletons and compared by reference.
 59→ */
 60→internal interface FieldSign<in Target> {
 61→    /**
 62→     * The field that is `true` if the value of the field is known to be negative, and `false` otherwise.
 63→     * Can be both read from and written to.
 64→     */
 65→    val isNegative: Accessor<Target, Boolean>
 66→
 67→    /**
 68→     * A check for whether the current value of the field is zero.
 69→     */
 70→    fun isZero(obj: Target): Boolean
 71→}
 72→
 73→/**
 74→ * A specification of a field.
 75→ *
 76→ * Fields represent parts of objects, regardless of how they are stored or formatted.
 77→ * For example, a field "day of week" can be represented with both strings of various kinds ("Monday", "Mon") and
 78→ * numbers ("1" for Monday, "2" for Tuesday, etc.), but the field itself is the same.
 79→ *
 80→ * Fields can typically contain `null` values, which means that the field is not set.
 81→ */
 82→internal interface FieldSpec<in Target, Type> {
 83→    /**
 84→     * The function with which the field can be accessed.
 85→     */
 86→    val accessor: Accessor<Target, Type>
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * The default value of the field, or `null` if the field has none.
 90→     */
 91→    val defaultValue: Type?
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * The name of the field.
 95→     */
 96→    val name: String
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * The sign corresponding to the field value, or `null` if the field has none.
100→     */
101→    val sign: FieldSign<Target>?
102→}
103→
104→/**
105→ * Inherit from this class to obtain a sensible [toString] implementation for debugging.
106→ */
107→internal abstract class AbstractFieldSpec<in Target, Type>: FieldSpec<Target, Type> {
108→    override fun toString(): String = "The field $name (default value is $defaultValue)"
109→}
110→
111→/**
112→ * A specification of a field that can contain values of any kind.
113→ * Used for fields additional information about which is not that important for parsing/formatting.
114→ */
115→internal class GenericFieldSpec<in Target, Type>(
116→    override val accessor: Accessor<Target, Type>,
117→    override val name: String = accessor.name,
118→    override val defaultValue: Type? = null,
119→    override val sign: FieldSign<Target>? = null,
120→) : AbstractFieldSpec<Target, Type>()
121→
122→/**
123→ * A specification of a field that can only contain non-negative numeric values.
124→ */
125→internal class UnsignedFieldSpec<in Target>(
126→    override val accessor: Accessor<Target, Int>,
127→    /**
128→     * The minimum value of the field.
129→     */
130→    val minValue: Int,
131→    /**
132→     * The maximum value of the field.
133→     */
134→    val maxValue: Int,
135→    override val name: String = accessor.name,
136→    override val defaultValue: Int? = null,
137→    override val sign: FieldSign<Target>? = null,
138→) : AbstractFieldSpec<Target, Int>() {
139→    /**
140→     * The maximum length of the field when represented as a decimal number.
141→     */
142→    val maxDigits: Int = when {
143→        maxValue < 10 -> 1
144→        maxValue < 100 -> 2
145→        maxValue < 1000 -> 3
146→        else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Max value $maxValue is too large")
147→    }
148→}
149→

 10→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
